A simple mechanic to implement using animation curves. An object such as a door or bridge has been broken into multiple parts, and the player has to use magic to rebuild it, visually reversing time. There will be puzzle and multiplayer opportunities. For instance one player may need to restore a bridge while another player crosses it to reach a switch. There can also be a multi-stage set of things that have collapsed or prematurely rusted. The player has to work out which ones to reverse, in which order, to recreate the original setting (all pieces lock into place once this has been achieved, for a short time).
Upgrade features form TA material

Only gliding for now. Gliding is an airborne locomotion state, not a flight simulation. While gliding, the player retains directional movement control similar to ground traversal, but with reduced acceleration, limited top speed, and continuous stamina drain.

The team says goodbye on chat and the party disbands as you step through.
